metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's three Expected variants are: (1) clean exit, (2) SIGKILL while idle in ready, and (3) SIGKILL mid-turn while still working. All three are now observed end-to-end for the codex adapter, recorded back-to-back via drive-codex-interactive.sh's `sigkill` + `restart` step primitives:

- Variant 1 (clean Ctrl-D exit): the rollout session is born in ready, the turn completes (working→ready), and process_exited fires after state returned to ready.
- Variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle): the turn completes back to ready, the rollout stays idle, and process_exited fires while in ready — no transition to working or waiting follows the turn.
- Variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn): the rollout enters working on the prompt and process_exited fires while still in working (~3 s after the working transition).

expected.jsonl encodes all three arcs (per-variant session_birth → handoff → turn_start [→ turn_done] → exit) and validates 14/14 phases against the recording.

## sigkill mechanism

Unlike claudecode (which pgreps `--session-id <uuid>` from argv), codex's argv is uniform (`codex --no-alt-screen` for every session), so there is no per-session argv marker. drive-codex-interactive.sh's step_sigkill instead targets the exact process the daemon tracks: codex holds its rollout .jsonl open for writing for the session lifetime, and the daemon discovers the PID as that write-FD holder (codex/pid.go → DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter, via lsof). The driver mirrors that lsof lookup, with a tmux-pane-tree fallback so a SIGKILL cannot merely orphan codex.
caveats: Variant 3 must sleep ~5 s before the kill (the recipe does), not the 2 s claudecode uses: codex's ready→working promotion lags ~2.7 s behind first activity, so a shorter window kills before working is observed and the variant collapses to ready→process_exited.

Variant 3's recording may or may not show a working→ready sweep before process_exited depending on the 8 s tail window — expected.jsonl asserts only that process_exited fires while in working (v3 has no turn_done phase).

sources.note: Daemon discovers the codex PID as the rollout's lsof write-FD holder — the target step_sigkill kills.
step_sigkill + step_restart primitives that make variants 2 and 3 recordable.

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: Driver creates 3 fresh cwds under <staging>/cwd, cwd-2, cwd-3 (one per variant); codex caches trust per directory, so each restart's fresh cwd shows its own trust dialog the driver auto-accepts
Codex assigns its own session_id per rollout — there is no preferred-UUID handoff like claudecode

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Write a 200 word essay about the history of coffee.

verify: events.jsonl contains exactly 3 distinct rollout session_ids (one per variant) plus their proc-<PID> pre-session rows
events.jsonl contains exactly 3 process_exited events, one per session_id
variant 1: the first session has a state_transition to ready right after its turn completes, immediately preceding its process_exited (clean Ctrl-D exit)
variant 2: the second session ends in ready (idle) when sigkill fires; no state_transition to working appears after its final ready

variant 3: the third session is in working when sigkill fires; irrlichd's sweep-promotes-to-ready behavior may or may not produce a working→ready transition before process_exited, depending on the 8 s tail window — accept either shape
the state band in the viewer shows three colored arcs separated by grey gaps where no session is alive between variants
recipe.scope_note: All three variants are recorded back-to-back via drive-codex-interactive.sh: (1) clean Ctrl-D exit after a no-op turn, (2) SIGKILL while idle in ready, (3) SIGKILL mid-turn while still working. restart starts a fresh codex (new rollout, fresh cwd) between variants; sigkill targets the daemon's PID directly (the rollout's lsof write-FD holder, per codex/pid.go) since codex's uniform argv has no per-session marker to pgrep. Variant 3 sleeps 5 s before the kill (vs claudecode's 2 s): codex's ready→working promotion lags ~2.7 s behind first activity, so a 2 s window kills BEFORE working is observed and the variant collapses to ready→process_exited. 5 s clears the promotion latency while staying mid-essay.
